title: Drop-in Activation Design: the MLP Battery
strategies: identity / linear table: echo the grid back so phi(x)=x -- collapses each net to a linear model and reproduces the evaluator's baseline (~0.1)
smooth saturating nonlinearity: tanh / sigmoid-shaped table introduces curvature so the nets bend decision boundaries, but vanishing tail gradients cap accuracy on the hard batteries
non-saturating rectifier: ReLU / leaky-ReLU keeps a live positive-side gradient under the shared GD schedule, training the tiny MLPs faster to higher geometric-mean accuracy
shaped / self-gated tables: GELU / SiLU-like or hand-tuned piecewise tables trading off saturation, negative-region leak and gradient magnitude across the diverse dataset kinds and the held-out net
